subject: Geography
title: Antarctica: The Ice Desert
description: A quick, practical guide to Antarctica: The Ice Desert.

hook.text: Antarctica holds 70% of Earth's fresh water, yet it's technically a desert.

buildup.text: The interior gets less than 50 mm of precipitation per year—drier than the Sahara.

discovery.text: It's the coldest, driest, and windiest continent. The record low is minus 89.2°C.

twist.text: The ice sheet is up to 4.8 km thick, pressing the bedrock below sea level.

climax.text: If all Antarctic ice melted, global sea levels would rise about 58 meters.

punchline.text: Earth's biggest desert holds Earth's biggest ice sheet.

quiz.question: Why is Antarctica classified as a desert?
options: It is covered in sand under the ice
